  "findings": [
    "Reported intermittent shaft alignment symptom was not captured — clip begins after symptom stopped",
    "No trend log, load reading, or repeat alignment test included to confirm failure mode",
    "Cannot distinguish reported misalignment from normal operational cycling without baseline data"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Obtain continuous vibration/trend monitoring data covering next occurrence of intermittent symptom",
    "Perform repeat laser alignment verification per API 686 and laser system manual procedures under representative load conditions",
    "Record load readings and thermal growth compensation data during alignment check",
    "Compare results against baseline alignment values and acceptable tolerance bands"
  ],
  "rationale": "The reported intermittent alignment issue cannot be diagnosed from the available evidence. The symptom had already stopped when observation began, and no trend logs, load readings, or repeat tests are provided. Without baseline data and a captured event, it is impossible to separate a genuine alignment fault from normal equipment cycling. API 686 requires documented alignment verification with repeatable measurements; the current record is insufficient to confirm or rule out a defect.",
  "references": [
    "API 686",
    "laser system manuals"
  ]
}
